message: renderReviewerPrompt still tells reviewers not to flag uncertain issues, contradicting the new candidate-state workflow.
evidence: The prompt emits "If you are not certain an issue is real, do not flag it" before later telling reviewers to use state="candidate" for likely real but not fully verified issues.

message: The reviewer prompt still tells reviewers not to report findings unless they are certain, which contradicts the contract's recall-first candidate reporting requirement.
evidence: The contract requires the prompt to no longer instruct reviewers to discard every uncertain candidate, but this line still emits: "If you are not certain an issue is real, do not flag it."

message: The new uncertainty field is persisted without repo-root sanitization.
evidence: Message, trigger, fix_direction, and evidence are sanitized before persistence, but Uncertainty is assigned directly from reviewer input; accepted proposals copy the same findingCore into findings, so absolute local paths in uncertainty can enter version-controlled review artifacts.

message: The prompt methodology test still requires the old certain-or-silent instruction, so it cannot catch the contract violation where reviewers are still told not to report uncertain candidates.
evidence: The test asserts the prompt contains "If you are not certain an issue is real, do not flag it" even though the contract requires recall-first candidate reporting.

message: The proposal acceptance tests do not exercise or assert accepted-finding carry-through for the new anti-false-positive fields.
evidence: appendReviewProposalForTest builds fixture proposals with only legacy findingCore fields; accept tests using it cannot fail if state, trigger, fix_direction, uncertainty, or current_code_only are dropped from accepted findings.

message: The generated reviewer prompt still tells reviewers to report findings only when certain, which contradicts the new recall-first candidate workflow.
evidence: renderReviewerPrompt still writes certain-only instructions at lines 1212-1213, while the same function later adds state=candidate guidance. The contract requires the prompt to stop discarding uncertain but tightly evidenced candidates.

message: The user-facing agents documentation still describes reviewer methodology as certain-or-silent and CONFIRMED-only, so it is stale after adding candidate findings and anti-false-positive fields.
evidence: docs/agents.md lines 557-562 describe certain-or-silent and CONFIRMED-only output, but the implemented prompt now supports candidate state, uncertainty, trigger, fix_direction, and current_code_only.
